title: Exploring The Rap Winners | GRAMMY.com
text: Grammy.com explores the history and legacy of Grammy rap winners. At the 54th Annual Grammy Awards, Chicago rapper and producer Kanye West achieved a historic sweep, winning all four rap categories. This matched a feat previously accomplished only by Eminem at the 43rd Annual GRAMMYs in 2000. West's victories included Best Rap Performance (Otis with Jay-Z), Best Rap/Sung Collaboration (All of the Lights featuring Rihanna, Kid Cudi, and Fergie), Best Rap Song (All of the Lights), and Best Rap Album (My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy). The article notes that West already had 14 GRAMMYs to his credit at that time. Modern Rap Dominance: The contemporary landscape shows Kendrick Lamar leading with 27 Grammy wins across 66 nominations. Jay-Z follows with 25 wins from 89 nominations, while Kanye West holds 24 wins from 76 nominations. The piece emphasizes that Hip-hop rappers, producers, and songwriters have significantly shaped the musical landscape since the genre first won GRAMMYs in 1989.
